mnemonics: BWP: Bounded? Well, Prove it! (A set with the Bolzano-Weierstrass property is sequentially compact, which in metric spaces implies it is closed and bounded.)
keyPoints: A metric space has the Bolzano-Weierstrass property if every infinite subset has a limit point.
In metric spaces, the Bolzano-Weierstrass property is equivalent to sequential compactness.
The property gets its name from the Bolzano-Weierstrass theorem in Rⁿ, which states every bounded infinite set has a limit point.
commonMistakes: Confusing the property with the Heine-Borel Theorem; B-W is about limit points, Heine-Borel is about open covers.
Assuming a set with a limit point is closed; a set can have limit points that lie outside itself (e.g., (0,1) has 0 as a limit point).
Forgetting that the property requires *every* infinite subset to have a limit point, not just one particular infinite subset.
